_note: Issue Match questions. Each question is tied to a REAL recorded council vote in votes.json via voteId. `agreeIs` says which side an 'Agree' answer lines up with: 'for' means agreeing matches councillors who voted FOR that motion. Every question here is worded as 'the city should [do the thing the motion proposed]', so agreeIs is 'for' throughout. Questions are chosen to be (a) understandable to a non-expert and (b) genuinely divided, so the result discriminates. Matching is computed in assets/js/match.js.

questions.text: Prince George should put more RCMP officers on the payroll, even though it raises costs.
questions.context: Jan 2025 budget: raising the RCMP's contracted strength from 153 to 157 officers (carried).

questions.text: The city should pay for extra RCMP overtime to increase patrols in struggling neighbourhoods like the Lower Patricia area.
questions.context: Apr 2023: an amendment directing RCMP funds toward more patrols in the Lower Patricia area (carried).

questions.text: The city should fund a dedicated Victim Services Worker to support victims of crime.
questions.context: Jan 2025 budget: adding a $92,116 Victim Services Worker position (the motion was defeated).

questions.text: The city should hire more bylaw-enforcement officers.
questions.context: Jan 2025 budget: adding two more Bylaw Services officers (the motion was defeated).

questions.text: The city should increase the Fire & Rescue budget.
questions.context: Jan 2025 budget: approving the Fire and Rescue Service Enhancement (carried).

questions.text: The city should spend public money to support a downtown revitalization program run by the downtown business association.
questions.context: Sep 2023: $20,000 from council contingency for a Downtown Vibrancy and Vitality Initiative (carried).

questions.text: The city should act to stop overnight camping in parks such as Millennium Park.
questions.context: May 2023: directing staff to begin stopping overnight camping at Millennium Park and remediating the site (carried).

questions.text: Council should reopen the bylaw that sets councillors' own pay and look at changing it.
questions.context: Jan 2024: directing staff to bring the Council Remuneration Bylaw back for review and possible amendment (the motion was defeated).

questions.text: Council should shrink its own discretionary 'contingency' spending fund.
questions.context: Jan 2025 budget: cutting the Council Contingency line from $75,000 to $50,000 (carried).

questions.text: The city should commit to a stable multi-year funding agreement with the public library.
questions.context: Jan 2025: directing staff to develop a 5-year funding agreement with the Prince George Public Library (carried).

questions.text: Council should demand a full financial breakdown before continuing to back a city-funded event.
questions.context: Apr 2023: directing staff to report back on the costs, revenue and free tickets of a past city-supported event (carried).

questions.text: The city should look into installing the 'Mr. PG' landmark at two new high-profile locations.
questions.context: Oct 2024: directing staff to report on installing Mr. PG at Canada Games Plaza and the Highway 16/97 junction (carried).
